questionHtml: Calculate the entropy change for the Haber–Bosch process, in J mol–1 K–1 at 298 K. Use your answer to (b)(i) and section 1 of the data booklet.
markschemeHtml:
ΔG = «ΔH – TΔS =» –93000 «J» – 298«K» × ΔS = –33000 ✔

ΔS = 〈〈-93000 J --33000 J298 K〉〉 = –201 «J mol–1 K–1» ✔

 

Do not penalize failure to convert kJ to J in both (c)(ii) and (c)(iii).

Award [2] for correct final answer

Award [1 max] for (+) 201 «J mol–1 K–1».

Award [2] for –101 or –100.5 «J mol–1 K–1».

meta.title: Calculate the entropy change for the Haber–Bosch process, in J mol–1 K–1 at 298 K. Use your answer to (b)(i) and section 1 of the data booklet.
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.4.2—Change in Gibbs energy, ΔG, relates the energy that can be obtained from a chemical reaction to the change in enthalpy, ΔH, change in entropy, ΔS, and absolute temperature, T. Apply the equation ΔG⦵ = ΔH⦵ − TΔS⦵ to calculate unknown values of these terms.
